summary: Frontend-only verification of the new autosave features (debounced 2s line/field autosave + HOVER apply autosave). Logged in as hhunt6677@yahoo.com, opened estimate EST-405137 (id 2fc4e383...), edited the Charter Oak Standard color Dutch Lap 4.5" .046 qty on the Vinyl tab. Validated 4 scenarios. Debounced autosave + coalescing + persistence after reload all WORK as specified. However, the pagehide/beforeunload sendBeacon flush on immediate navigate-away did NOT persist the change — the value returned to the previously-saved one. HOVER importer UI elements are present with correct data-testids; full HOVER apply-then-reload could not be exercised because no real HOVER PDF fixture was available.

integration_issues.flow: Autosave on tab close / navigate-away (pagehide+beforeunload sendBeacon flush)
integration_issues.issue: Typed qty=13 on Charter Oak Standard color Dutch Lap 4.5" .046, waited ~200ms (well within 2s debounce), then navigated to /dashboard/siding via page.goto. Reopened the same estimate — the qty came back as 7 (the prior persisted value), NOT 13. The sendBeacon flush in useEstimate.js (lines 267-297) is not actually persisting the in-flight edit. Likely causes worth checking: (a) sendBeacon Blob with content-type 'application/json' may be rejected by FastAPI CORS preflight or auth, (b) the axios api client may attach auth via Authorization header (not cookies), so sendBeacon — which only sends cookies — arrives unauthenticated and the PUT is 401'd silently, (c) api.defaults.baseURL may already include trailing path that mis-composes the URL. Test A and Test B both passed (regular 2s debounce + reload works perfectly), so the debounce path itself is fine — only the synchronous-flush path on pagehide is broken.
affected_selectors: qty-Vinyl Siding-Charter Oak Standard color Dutch Lap 4.5" .046

action_items: Investigate the pagehide/beforeunload sendBeacon flush in /app/frontend/src/lib/useEstimate.js (lines 267-297). Likely cause: auth header is set on the axios api client but sendBeacon only sends cookies — so the PUT arrives unauthenticated. Recommended fixes to try in order: (1) verify auth is cookie-based; if Bearer token is in Authorization header, sendBeacon will not include it — use fetch({keepalive:true, credentials:'include', headers:{Authorization:...}}) instead of sendBeacon, OR (2) keep sendBeacon but additionally store the token in an httpOnly cookie that the backend accepts. Reproduce by: open editor → type qty → IMMEDIATELY page.goto away within ~500ms → return → qty is lost.

OPTIONAL: when a HOVER PDF test fixture is available, exercise: upload → Apply N Lines & Save → confirm toast contains '· saved' and reload without manual Save preserves all imported lines (covers the HOVER autosave-on-apply path which we could only static-verify in code).
critical_code_review_comments: useEstimate.js:259-263 — debounce useEffect is keyed on [userEdits, autosave]. Since autosave is a useCallback memoised on [id, buildPayload] and buildPayload is memoised on [], autosave reference is stable. Good. But: if the user edits, then navigates to a different estimate id (same component remount), the cleanup runs and timer is cleared — safe.

useEstimate.js:281 — `if (navigator.sendBeacon && navigator.sendBeacon(url, blob)) return;` — sendBeacon returns true synchronously if the browser QUEUED the request, not if the server accepted. Auth failures (401) are completely silent here. This is the most plausible reason TEST C fails. Add a console.warn fallback path or stop relying on sendBeacon for authenticated requests.

useEstimate.js:243 — `if (savingRef.current) return;` in autosave skips when a save is already in flight. That means if the user edits during a save, the new value won't be queued for another save. Consider re-running autosave after savingRef flips to false (or rely on userEdits effect to retrigger — it does, on next edit, so probably fine but worth a comment).

HoverImportButton.jsx:108-118 — calls `update({lines: next})` AND `save({...est, lines: next})` in sequence. The update() bumps userEdits which schedules a second autosave 2s later (mentioned as 'redundant but harmless' in problem statement). This DOES cause two PUTs per HOVER apply — acceptable for now but could be optimised by suppressing the autosave bump when save() was just called.

useEstimate.js:243 — savingRef is set true in autosave but the explicit save() also sets it (line 222). If save() is called while autosave is mid-flight (e.g. user hits Save), the savingRef.current is already true and autosave was already running — save() will run anyway and the two PUTs may interleave. Low-risk race; consider awaiting in flight or using a queue.

success_rate.frontend: 75% (3 of 4 autosave scenarios PASS; pagehide-flush FAILS)

seed_data_creation: none — used existing estimate EST-405137 (id 2fc4e383-1ef9-423d-b756-df4fc4dfdb47). NOTE: this test mutated the Charter Oak Dutch Lap .046 qty to 7. Reset to 0 if a clean state is needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Editor route is /estimate/{id} (singular), NOT /estimates/{id}. Estimate list rows use data-testid='estimate-row-{uuid}'. The Charter Oak Dutch Lap .046 qty input testid is exactly `qty-Vinyl Siding-Charter Oak Standard color Dutch Lap 4.5" .046` — note the embedded double-quote, so use page.get_by_test_id(...) (NOT a CSS attribute selector, which fails to parse). Save button has data-testid='save-btn'. HOVER importer testids: hover-import-btn, hover-import-input, hover-modal, hover-apply-btn, hover-cancel-btn, hover-modal-close, hover-modal-backdrop. Section accordion uses section-{title} and subcat-toggle-{id} (e.g. subcat-toggle-charter). Charter subcat is collapsed by default — must click subcat-toggle-charter before its inputs are queryable. PUT /api/estimates/{id} is the autosave endpoint.

verified_acceptance_criteria.debounce_coalesces_back_to_back_edits: PASS — two edits within 0.4s produced exactly 1 PUT (final value 7), not 2
verified_acceptance_criteria.navigate_away_flush_via_sendBeacon: FAIL — typing qty=13 then immediately navigating away did not persist; reverted to 7 on return
verified_acceptance_criteria.hover_importer_button_visible_with_testi: PASS — hover-import-btn rendered, enabled, hover-import-input file input present
verified_acceptance_criteria.hover_apply_autosave_end_to_end: NOT TESTED — no HOVER PDF fixture available; static code review confirms apply() awaits save({...est, lines: next}) before closing modal and shows Saving… spinner via applying state

verified_acceptance_criteria.console_errors: PASS — none observed during the run

rca of the issue: TEST C failure RCA: The synchronous flush on pagehide/beforeunload uses navigator.sendBeacon. sendBeacon: (1) only includes cookies (not Authorization headers set by axios at runtime), (2) returns true if the browser queues the request — NOT if the server accepts it. If the app authenticates with a Bearer token via axios interceptor, the beacon PUT arrives unauthenticated and the server returns 401, but sendBeacon swallows that result. The fallback fetch({keepalive:true, credentials:'include'}) is only reached when sendBeacon returns false (queue failure), not on a 401 response. Reproduction: edit qty in editor → call window.location.href='/dashboard/siding' within 1.5s → return → value lost. Mitigation: switch the pagehide handler to use fetch({keepalive:true}) with the axios-style auth header attached explicitly; OR make the API treat the auth cookie as primary so sendBeacon works.
